desk.rationale: Risk clean, research agrees, but signal flags overextension below VWAP; reduced size.
desk.plan: Enter META at 612.97 with a stop at 563.93 and first target at 711.05 (2.0 R:R). The thesis is simple: strong catalyst-driven momentum in a risk-on regime with RSI still in healthy territory. The concern is real though — the 8.9% one-day rip with a wick to entry suggests you're buying an extension. Go quarter-size only and watch for a reclaim of VWAP; if it rolls back under 600 with volume, that's your early exit cue before the stop.

signal.note: Up 8.86% 1d, 9.9% 5d. RSI 58.8 healthy but spark chart shows extreme wick to 612.97—likely extension/profit-taking zone. Below VWAP.

research.note: Fresh headline + strong technicals. 8.9% move, 2.6x vol justifies catalyst review.

risk.concern: Strong momentum, risk-on regime, RSI neutral. Clean long setup.

desk.rationale: Signal and risk agree; research neutral but not opposed. Solid technical setup, low risk.
desk.plan: Buy NTNX at 51.20, stop at 47.10, target 59.39 (2.0 R:R). This is a clean technical momentum trade — MACD bullish cross, RSI at a comfortable 59, and the 5-day trend intact without the chasing risk of a big 1-day spike. Research is lukewarm on lack of catalyst, so keep it half-size. The setup breaks down if price slips back below 47.10, signaling the momentum run has stalled.

desk.plan: Enter GEN at 26.11, stop at 24.02, target 30.29 (2.0 R:R). The 5-day trend of +10.85% is the steadiest climb in the batch — not a one-day spike, a real ascent — and RSI at 63 leaves room to run. Half-size because research can't pin a catalyst. The trade is wrong if GEN loses the 24.02 level, which would suggest the move was purely short-covering and is now exhausted.
